title: Quassia warmingiana
taxonomy: Quassia warmingiana is a species of plant in the family Simaroubaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the quassia or sandalwood family. The Simaroubaceae family is part of the order Sapindales, which also includes other families such as the Anacardiaceae and the Rutaceae. Quassia warmingiana is a member of the genus Quassia, which consists of tropical plants native to the Americas. The classification of Quassia warmingiana is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its distinctive leaves and bark. Understanding the classification of Quassia warmingiana helps botanists and researchers to better understand its evolutionary history and relationships to other plant species.

intro: Quassia amara, not Quassia warmingiana, is a plant species known scientifically as Picrasma excelsa and Quassia amara is Quassia amara's synonym, however Quassia amara is known as the Quassia or quassia wood or Quassia amara.
